author: Austin Crawford
title: Documentary Film Screening
summary: From Award-Winning Documentary Filmmaker Fredrick Lewis, Paul Laurence Dunbar: Beyond the Mask is a documentary on the life and legacy of the first African American to achieve national fame as a writer. Join us for a screening and discussion on Saturday, September 10th at 1p.m.
content:
From Award-Winning Documentary Filmmaker Fredrick Lewis, Paul Laurence Dunbar: Beyond the Mask is a documentary on the life and legacy of the first African American to achieve national fame as a writer. Join us for a screening and discussion on Saturday, September 10th at 1p.m.

Born to former slaves in Dayton, Ohio, Paul Laurence Dunbar (1872-1906), is best remembered for his poem, “We Wear the Mask” and for lines from “Sympathy” that became the title of Maya Angelou’s famous autobiography “I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings.” A clip of Angelou reciting Dunbar’s poem on the David Frost Show is featured. Dunbar’s story is also the story of the African American experience around the turn of the century.

For tickets to this free screening click the link below.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Learn How To Prepare Nutritious Meals
summary: Learn how to prepare nutritious and flavorful meals on a budget and decrease your risk of obesity and related chronic
content:
Learn how to prepare nutritious and flavorful meals on a budget and decrease your risk of obesity and related chronic
diseases through food and diet changes. This is a free, four-week virtual course through Zoom (from at home or in the library) in conjunction with Lifespan Community Health. Participants will receive a free cookbook and bottle of extra virgin olive oil. Register in advance at https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/FIM52022QGBBYK2.
Tuesday, May 17 at 7 p.m.: “Andy Irons: Kissed by a God,” a Teton Gravity film. A Soundwaves production for May Mental Health Awareness Month.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Block Island Conservancy Fair
summary: The Conservancy’s purpose is to protect Block Island’s natural heritage, rural character, and access to its resources. This is accomplished primarily by preserving land as open space through acquisition or via conservation easement.
content:
The Conservancy’s purpose is to protect Block Island’s natural heritage, rural character, and access to its resources. This is accomplished primarily by preserving land as open space through acquisition or via conservation easement.

Help celebrate and raise money for the land preservation. This day is all about kids and nature - come have some fun!  This a wonderful event for children of all ages.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Poor People's Pub Season Opener
summary: The best way to kick off the carefree days and nights of summer is a visit to Block Island. And the best way to celebrate is to head to Poor People’s Pub for their annual weekend Season Opener on June 1, 2 and 3. That’s three straight days of food, music, drinks, and dancing.
content:
The best way to kick off the carefree days and nights of summer is a visit to Block Island. And the best way to celebrate is to head to Poor People’s Pub for their annual weekend Season Opener on June 1, 2 and 3. That’s three straight days of food, music, drinks, and dancing.

Hot music with Pub fave DJ Libre gets you in the groove starting Friday night at 10 pm. On Saturday afternoon from 1 to 4 pm, it’s time for the annual epic pig roast on the patio, with a side of sounds from Rhythmic Circus. Just follow your nose down Ocean Avenue to the heavenly aroma of barbecued pork, a Poor People’s Pub specialty. At 10 pm, hustle back to the dance floor because DJ Libre returns to kick up the vibe for another blowout dance party.

You’ll want to take things easy on Sunday, and PPP has your back with its famous Sunday Rehab Brunch starting at 11:30 am. Now that you have your second wind, stick around for Jazz Grooves playing on the patio from 1 to 4 pm and end the weekend on a high note. 

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

meta.title: Poor People Pub Season Opener - Pig Roast
meta.description: Poor People’s Pub annual weekend Season Opener on June 1, 2 and 3. That’s three straight days of food, music, drinks, and dancing.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Shad Bloom 10K Run
summary: This spring race (an island favorite) takes runners over a scenic 10K course with breathtaking views of beautiful Block Island.
content:
This spring race (an island favorite) takes runners over a scenic 10K course with breathtaking views of beautiful Block Island. 

The race includes a little bit of everything: dirt roads with stone walls and rolling pastures, a winding track overlooking the Atlantic, and at mile 4 racers meet "the wall", a steep, double tiered, narrow climb.

Every mile is different. This race has a loyal following. 

The race starts at 1 pm.  The start and end are at the Block Island School. 

Packet pickup/ day of registration will be at the Block Island School starting at 9 am the day of the race. Bus transportation will meet the 9 am Point Judith ferry (arrives on Block Island 10 .am.)

Register on Active.com 

Questions? Please call 401-466-3223 and ask for Dave or Cindy. Or send us an email at recreation@new-shoreham.com.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Lion's Club Clambake
summary: Lobsters and clams and mussels, oh my. And chowder and corn on the cob and sausage, too. It wouldn't be Labor Day on Block Island without the annual Lion's Club Clambake on Saturday, September 5th starting at 1 pm. It all takes place under a tent on the grounds of the charming and historic Narragansett Inn, where you'll enjoy sweeping views of the ocean and the boats bobbing in New Harbor.
content:
Lobsters and clams and mussels, oh my. And chowder and corn on the cob and sausage, too. It wouldn't be Labor Day on Block Island without the annual Lion's Club Clambake on Saturday, September 5th starting at 1 pm. It all takes place under a tent on the grounds of the charming and historic Narragansett Inn, where you'll enjoy sweeping views of the ocean and the boats bobbing in New Harbor. 

Not a shellfish lover? No worries. The Lions put out a fine spread for the landlubbers too, with yummy hamburgers, hotdogs, and sausage for the carnivores in the crowd.

The Lion's Club is a group that mixes socializing and service, doing lots of good for the Block Island community all year long. These hard-working folks plan this fundraiser for months in advance, so come on out and bid a fond farewell to the summer with food and fun, music and merriment, raffle drawings and more.

Not a shellfish lover? No worries. The Lions put out a fine spread for the landlubbers too, with yummy hamburgers, hot dogs, and sausage for the carnivores in the crowd.

author: Austin Crawford
title: 19th Annual 5K Turkey Trot
summary: Come and enjoy an awesome event on Block Island. This 5K is timed perfectly so you can enjoy the holiday festivities on the island. You can run or walk whichever you choose. The start and finish line is at the Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion on Corn Neck Rd. The race starts at 1:30 pm.
content:
Come and enjoy an awesome event on Block Island. This 5K is timed perfectly so you can enjoy the holiday festivities on the island. You can run or walk whichever you choose. The start and finish line is at the Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion on Corn Neck Rd. The race starts at 1:30 pm.

To register click here

title: The Block Island Early Learning Center Annual Toy Boat fundraiser
summary: The toy boat race will be held on September 22, 2025 at 1:30pm @ the bridge below the police station on Ocean Ave (next to Paynes killer donuts!) All proceeds will benefit The Block Island Early Learning Center. $10 per  boat.
content:
The toy boat race will be held on September 22, 2025 at 1:30pm @ the bridge below the police station on Ocean Ave (next to Paynes killer donuts!) All proceeds will benefit The Block Island Early Learning Center. $10 per  boat.  

First Place $500 Cash

Second Place $200 Winfield's Gift Card

Third Place $100 Diamond Blue Gift Card

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: 2nd Annual Free the Fish Event!
summary: FREE means so many different things, but the real "FREE" is happening this Sunday, October 4th at 1:30 PM at The Block Island Maritime Institute. Join us then for our second annual Free the Fish event!
content:
FREE means so many different things, but the real "FREE" is happening this Sunday, October 4th at 1:30 PM at The Block Island Maritime Institute. Join us then for our second annual Free the Fish event!

We'll feed the fish and shortly after, we'll set them FREE to return to their natural habitat in the Great Salt Pond and beyond.

author: Analyn Alforque
title: MOVIE NIGHT THIS WEEK - SATURDAY 9/22
summary: Watch the Truman Show at the Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion! An amazing film, a Golden Globe winning performance by the incomparable Jim Carrey.
content: Watch the Truman Show at the Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion! An amazing film, a Golden Globe winning performance by the incomparable Jim Carrey.

author: Will Gasner
title: Movie Night At The Town Beach
summary: Tonight @ Fred Benson Town Beach...
content:
Tonight @ Fred Benson Town Beach...

E.T. The Extra-Terrestrial 
(Rated PG, 1982)

FREE* ALL AGES!!!

Showtime is at sundown, which has typically been between 8:10-8:20pm.  Get there earlier to snag a good spot, though!   Josh plays music starting @7:30. 

Talk about The Perfect Tuesday:  After you go to Berke Marye's Art Opening @ Spring Street Gallery, head down to the beach pavilion with your Beach Chairs & Blankets (feels like a sweater night, too!) for E.T. on the big screen at the beach!  🏻

Last week was our biggest turnout yet and we're expecting a great one this week, too!

*Donations Encouraged

author: Austin Crawford
title: Film & Soup at Island Free Library
summary: Film & Soup is a collaboration between The Nature Conservancy and Island Free Library. Each winter month an environmental film and soup are served for the collective enjoyment of all.  Bring a spoon & cup/bowl to share in the soup.
content:
Film & Soup is a collaboration between The Nature Conservancy and Island Free Library. Each winter month an environmental film and soup are served for the collective enjoyment of all.  Bring a spoon & cup/bowl to share in the soup.

 

On December 30th at 2 pm, join us at the Island Free Library for the movie viewing:” The Lorax.” “The Lorax” is a 2012 animated musical fantasy film based on Dr. Seuss’s beloved children’s book, featuring a vibrant story about environmentalism

and the importance of trees.

 

This is a FREE event. All Welcome!!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Book signing: The Law Of Wellness at The Island Free Library
summary: Join Ann Law, Global Health & Wellness Expert and author of the #1 Amazon best seller The Law of Wellness, for an inspiring talk, audience Q&A, and book signing. Discover practical principles to reduce stress, elevate energy, and create everyday wellbeing.
content:
Join Ann Law, Global Health & Wellness Expert and author of the #1 Amazon best seller The Law of Wellness, for an inspiring talk, audience Q&A, and book signing. Discover practical principles to reduce stress, elevate energy, and create everyday wellbeing.

 

Click on the image for more information.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Yoga workshop at Island Free Library
summary: Class lll Relax. This will be a restorative class that uses breathwork and restorative poses—integrating some of the tools we have learned thus far—to foster to foster deep relaxation. Please bring pillows and blankets. Room B downstairs.
content: Class lll Relax. This will be a restorative class that uses breathwork and restorative poses—integrating some of the tools we have learned thus far—to foster to foster deep relaxation. Please bring pillows and blankets. Room B downstairs.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Soft-shell Clam Project - Nature Walk and Community Science
summary: Block Island hosts many cool community science projects. Join the fun, different subject and location each week, and help collect important data about Block Island’s special critters and habitats.
content:
Block Island hosts many cool community science projects. Join the fun, different subject and location each week, and help collect important data about Block Island’s special critters and habitats. 

 

 

Advance sign-up recommended but not required, Email kim.gaffett@tnc.org

content:

National Hotel - 2 time options - 9am and 2pm

title: Darik And The Funbags at Ballard's
summary: Get ready for a night of pure fun with Darik and The Funbags at Ballard’s Beach Resort! This lively band is known for delivering energetic performances and playing all your favorite tunes that are guaranteed to get you on your feet and dancing. From classic hits to modern favorites, Darik and The Funbags bring the party wherever they go. Join us for an unforgettable day of music, laughter, and good times with Darik and The Funbags at Ballard’s!

author: Austin Crawford
title: RI Hospitality Association Training Class Servsafe® Alcohol Certification
summary: SERVSAFE® ALCOHOL CERTIFICATION
content:
SERVSAFE® ALCOHOL CERTIFICATION
Anyone involved in the selling or service of alcohol (restaurant/bar/hotel staff, managers &
supervisors, retailers, and curbside/door-to-door delivery persons) are required by law to be
Alcohol Service Certified. Upon successful completion, this certification is valid in the
United States for 3 years.
 

TO REGISTER FOR SERVSAFE® ALCOHOL PLEASE CONTACT: emma@blockislandinfo.com | 401.466.5200 

When?
TUESDAY, JUNE 25TH | 2-4:30PM at The National Hotel

author: Austin Crawford
title: Pirate Weekend Lil' Bucs Crafts and Activities
summary: Direct your sail to under the tent for fun pirate themed crafts and activities!
content: Direct your sail to under the tent for fun pirate themed crafts and activities!

author: Austin Crawford
title: 2024 Storm Trysail Club Block Island Race Preliminary
summary: 82 boats are gearing up for the 77th Annual Block Island Race, set to commence this Friday, (May 24) at 2 p.m. off Stamford, CT. Teams will embark on the challenging 186-nautical mile course, navigating through Long Island Sound, around Block Island, and back. The weather forecast looks to be typical for this time of year. With a late sea breeze from the southwest and an outgoing tide predicted for the first afternoon, sailors will hope for the breeze to fill to get them going out before the tide turns and the breeze turns back to the north.
content: 82 boats are gearing up for the 77th Annual Block Island Race, set to commence this Friday, (May 24) at 2 p.m. off Stamford, CT. Teams will embark on the challenging 186-nautical mile course, navigating through Long Island Sound, around Block Island, and back. The weather forecast looks to be typical for this time of year. With a late sea breeze from the southwest and an outgoing tide predicted for the first afternoon, sailors will hope for the breeze to fill to get them going out before the tide turns and the breeze turns back to the north.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Zach Mccabe at Ballard's
summary: Experience the soulful sounds of Zach Mccabe at Ballard’s Beach Resort, straight from the heart of Nashville, Tennessee! With his captivating voice and heartfelt lyrics, Zach brings a touch of Southern charm and musical prowess to our shores. Join us for an unforgettable evening of live music as Zach serenades you with his original songs and classic covers. Don’t miss this chance to witness the talent of Zach Mccabe live at Ballard’s!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Those Guys at Ballard's
summary: Get ready to groove the night away with Those Guys at Ballard’s Beach Resort this May! Back by popular demand, this energetic party dance band is a favorite among Ballard’s guests, and they’re returning to rock the stage once again. With their infectious beats, soulful melodies, and irresistible charm, Those Guys are sure to keep the party going all night long. Join us for an unforgettable evening filled with great music, dancing, and endless fun!
